description: Satire requires characters trapped in or benefiting from the satirized system, institutional settings that mirror real-world targets, and comic beats that serve the critique — removing the joke removes the point.

character_constraints.description: A character who enacts the satirized ideology sincerely — their earnestness reveals the absurdity.
A character who navigates the system cynically for personal gain — knows the rules are absurd but exploits them.
A newcomer or foreigner whose confusion reveals the system's absurdity to the audience.
A character whose suffering makes the critique concrete — without victims, satire becomes mere cleverness.

relationship_constraints.description: Characters are trapped in, benefiting from, or perpetuating the satirized system — no one is fully outside it.

place_constraints.description: A political body, bureaucracy, corporation, or social institution that serves as a legible parallel to the real-world target.

object_constraints.description: Forms, policies, documents, or procedures that embody bureaucratic dysfunction or ideological absurdity.
Reports, products, or outcomes that demonstrate the gap between institutional rhetoric and reality.

element_rules.description: Every comic beat must serve the satirical critique — removing the joke removes the point. Comedy without critique is not satire.
element_rules.testable_condition: Each comic scene in the timeline targets a specific aspect of the satirized system or ideology.

element_rules.description: The satirical world's absurd rules must be applied consistently throughout — internal logic must hold even as it exposes external absurdity.
element_rules.testable_condition: Rules established in the first quarter of the timeline are enforced consistently through the climax.
